summary: Unauthenticated attacker accesses Traefik v1.7 /api endpoint and retrieves full configuration including backend URLs, internal IPs, and routing rules
expected_security_impact: Full infrastructure visibility including backend service URLs, internal IPs, frontend routing rules, load balancer configurations, and potentially credentials - enabling targeted attacks on discovered services
actual_observed_result: API endpoint /api responds without authentication and returns JSON configuration containing backend_urls, internal_ips, and internal_domains fields with sensitive infrastructure details
verification_method: Automated checks in verify.sh confirm presence of sensitive data patterns (URLs, IPs, domains) in API response, exploit.py output validated for configuration disclosure
evidence_produced_by_run: exposed_data.json contains full Traefik configuration with backend service URLs
Internal IP addresses (e.g., 10.0.0.x, 192.168.x.x ranges) present in API response
Backend URLs and routing rules accessible without any authentication
API responds to unauthenticated GET requests on port 8080

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
